violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2026

The areas behind shelves and under equipment has an accumulation of, pest droppings soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to continue with increased pest control. Establishment is to clean and sanitize any and all droppings. Establishment is to send pest control reports to RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026
Rice was observed adulterated. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (bone saw) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings by the area for rice storage. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of cleaning and sanitizing. Establishment is to increase pest control. Establishment is to have pest control do a full evaluation of establishment. Establishment is to have all pest control finding available to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Owner arrived during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2023

Gnawed bags of rice observed in the market. FOOD packages shall be in good condition and protect the integrity of the contents so that the FOOD is not exposed to ADULTERATION or potential contaminants. Rice was embargoed until a dumpster can be provided for disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (band saw) were observed with an accumulation of rodent droppings and food debris. Food debris was observed on the meat grinder. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The kitchen is closed, and is not to be used for any food preparation or packaging until approval is given to reopen. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

Cooked tripe and bamboo observed stored open at room temperature. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items are embargoed until a dumpster is provided for disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2023
Rodent bait shall be contained in a covered, tamper-resistant bait station. Panular rodent bait observed beneath pallets of rice in the market. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2023

Produce and other foods packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels, or have incomplete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by dead rodents, and rodent droppings throughout the establishment including in and on the produce display cooler, on packaged produce, on market shelves, in boxes of packaged foods on market shelves, on the kitchen hand sink, single service storage shelf, meat band saw, plastic wrap machine, etc. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control service unknown by PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
Food observed stored on the floor in the market, and on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean (on shelf with rodent droppings) in the kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

Cardboard and newspaper observed lining shelves in the kitchen and market. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
Drain plugs are lacking for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2023
Walls are in poor repair, with rodent holes observed along the floor in the front of the market. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

Sleeping quarters observed upstairs.Living or sleeping quarters located on the PREMISES of a FOOD ESTABLISHMENT such as those provided for lodging registration clerks or resident managers shall be separated from rooms and areas used for FOOD ESTABLISHMENT operations by complete partitioning and solid self-closing doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of rodent droppings, soil residue and/or debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
